segments.text: I'm Olya, and today we're going to learn some really fun Russian words.
Today we are talking about the weather.
Our word of the day is pagoda.
That means what is the weather like today?

It is like the English word solar.
The sun gives us solar power.

Now a little about how Russians talk about the weather.
In Russian we can start a sentence with just the feeling word.
We say, холодно, when we mean it is cold.
We do not always need to add, it is.
It is shorter and feels like a quick friendly weather report.
So instead of a long sentence you can simply say, жарко.
That means it is hot right now.
Or you can add a time word and say, завтра будет тепло.
You already know these words so you can make real sentences.
Русские часто говорят о погоде, потому что их страна очень большая.
Russians talk about the weather a lot because their country is very big.
It stretches from freezing Arctic winters all the way to hot summers in the south.
You will often hear friends greet each other with нукак погода.
Even if they saw each other yesterday.
It is a warm friendly way to start a chat and shows how much they notice changes in nature.
Хотите узнать секрет, об одном из наших слов.
Want to know a secret about one of our words?
Погода and the English word weather are actually distant cousins.
They both come from a very old route that meant to make wet or to blow.
This route traveled through different ancient languages.
It became weather in English and погода in Russian.
Along the way the exact meaning changed a little.
In Russian погода now describes the whole state of the sky and temperature for the day.
Погода sounds a bit like powder, but it has nothing to do with snow powder.
Next time you check the weather forecast, remember this family connection.
That ancient wet and windy route is your secret memory hook forever.

Point to each picture and say the Russian word three times.
